subject: english
learning_objectives: Distinguish meanings of words that share the same spelling

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Homographs are words that are spelt the same but differ in meaning, origin, or pronunciation.

sections.heading: Examples
sections.content:
bow (verb) = to bend the head or upper body forward; bow (noun) = a weapon for shooting arrows.
minute (noun) = one of the sixty parts of an hour; minute (adjective) = very small.
convict (verb) = to declare someone guilty in court; convict (noun) = a person found guilty of a crime.
record (noun) = an account kept for reference; record (verb) = to keep a permanent account of facts or events.
perfect (adjective) = without fault; perfect (verb) = to make something perfect.
exercise (noun) = physical or mental activity; exercise (verb) = to use power, right, or personal qualities.
found (verb) = past tense of find; found (verb) = to establish an organisation.
